title: Christmas-Eve, Another Ceremony
content:
Come guard this night the Christmas-Pie,
That the thief, though ne'er so sly,
With his flesh-hooks, don't come nigh
To catch it

From him, who all alone sits there,
Having his eyes still in his ear,
And a deal of nightly fear
To watch it.
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, Robert Herrick delivers a powerful performance in "Christmas-Eve, Another Ceremony"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
